data.title: Google Expands Immersive Navigation Across the U.S.
data.description: Google has announced the full rollout of its immersive navigation feature in the United States starting today. According to Jin10, the tech giant plans to gradually extend the coverage of this feature over the coming months. This development marks a significant step in enhancing user experience by providing more interactive and detailed navigation options. The expansion is expected to improve accessibility and convenience for users across various regions.

data.title: Japan and U.S. to Enhance Confidential Information Sharing
data.description: Japan and the United States have agreed to expand their sharing of confidential information, according to Jin10. This move aims to strengthen bilateral cooperation and enhance security measures between the two nations. The agreement reflects ongoing efforts to address mutual concerns and improve strategic communication. Details regarding the specific areas of information sharing and the implementation timeline have not been disclosed. The initiative underscores the importance of collaboration in addressing global security challenges.

data.title: Robinhood launches GRT
data.description: According to the official page, Robinhood has launched GRT.

data.title: Singapore Sentences Zhang Xinghua for Cryptocurrency Theft
data.description: Singapore has sentenced Zhang Xinghua to two years in prison for his involvement in a conspiracy that resulted in the theft of approximately $6.9 million in cryptocurrency from SafeX. According to NS3.AI, Zhang transferred over $1.6 million in cryptocurrency into Tornado Cash through two separate transactions. Authorities have managed to seize or freeze around $2.1 million, but approximately $4.8 million remains beyond Singapore's jurisdiction.

data.title: Robinhood launches W
data.description: According to the official page, Robinhood has launched W.

data.title: EU Leaders Advocate for Reduced Dependency on Foreign Payment Systems
data.description: The European Union's major economies are calling for a decrease in reliance on external payment systems and a faster implementation of the digital euro. Bloomberg posted on X that these measures are aimed at enhancing productivity and growth within Europe. The push for a digital euro is part of a broader strategy to strengthen the EU's financial autonomy and competitiveness in the global market. Leaders believe that reducing dependency on foreign systems will provide more stability and control over economic transactions. The initiative is seen as crucial for the EU's long-term economic strategy, as it seeks to position itself as a leader in digital finance.

data.title: Eightco Holdings secured $125 million in funding commitments, with Bitmine set to lead a $75 million round.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Eightco Holdings announced a new $125 million funding round, with Ethereum treasury firm Bitmine leading the round with $75 million, ARK Invest committing at least $25 million, and Payward, the parent company of crypto platform Kraken, also committing $25 million to ORBS. The new funds will support its business expansion, investing in technologies shaping next-generation artificial intelligence, blockchain infrastructure, and global digital consumption platforms. The company also stated it will continue to hold shares in Worldcoin, co-founded by Sam Altman, and Ethereum, and remains bullish on the ERC-20 protocol in the long term. Bitmine Chairman Tom Lee will reportedly join the Eightco Holdings board of directors. ARK Invest Chief Futurist Brett Winton will serve as an advisor to the ORBS board, while Dan Ives will step down as chairman of ORBS. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Volkswagen and Volvo Warn of Middle East Conflict Impact on Demand
data.description: Volkswagen and Volvo have expressed concerns that the ongoing conflict in the Middle East could negatively impact demand. Bloomberg posted on X that this warning comes as automakers are already facing challenges in recovering from inconsistent electric vehicle sales, tariffs, and a downturn in the Chinese market. The automotive industry is navigating a complex landscape, and the potential effects of geopolitical tensions add another layer of uncertainty to their recovery efforts.

data.title: BlackRock's first Ethereum ETF that supports staking is about to be listed and traded on Nasdaq.
data.description: Asset management giant BlackRock launched its staking-enabled Ethereum ETF, the iShares Staked Ethereum Trust ETF (ETHB), which will begin trading on Nasdaq on Thursday. The fund will hold spot Ethereum and stake a portion of its assets on the network, allowing investors to gain exposure to ETH prices while earning staking rewards. ETHB is BlackRock's third crypto ETF and its first to introduce a staking mechanism. Previously, the company launched the iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) and the iShares Ethereum Trust (ETHA), with IBIT managing over $55 billion in assets and ETHA approximately $6.5 billion. BlackRock stated that the introduction of staking functionality aims to meet the needs of some investors who want to earn returns while gaining exposure to ETH prices, while retaining the traditional financial advantages of ETFs in custody, trading, and asset allocation. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Lloyds Investigates Technical Glitch in Mobile App
data.description: Lloyds Banking Group is currently investigating a technical issue that has resulted in some users being able to view other customers' payment details on its mobile application. Bloomberg posted on X that the bank is working to resolve the problem and ensure the security of its customers' information. The glitch has raised concerns about data privacy and security among users. Lloyds has assured customers that it is taking the matter seriously and is committed to maintaining the integrity of its services.

data.title: Binance wins another anti-terrorism lawsuit in the US; Alabama federal court dismisses all charges.
data.description: Binance has announced another victory in a lawsuit related to the U.S. Anti-Terrorism Act (ATA). A U.S. federal court in Alabama recently dismissed all charges against Binance, finding the plaintiffs' lawsuit flawed both legally and factually, and deeming the complaint "shotgun pleading" for failing to clearly distinguish the specific responsibilities of each defendant. The court allowed the plaintiffs to file a revised complaint by April 10, 2026, otherwise the case will be completely dismissed. This ruling follows a previous ruling by the Southern District of New York federal court, in which 535 plaintiffs accused Binance of supporting 64 terrorist attacks, but the court ultimately found the plaintiffs failed to prove the core allegations. Binance stated that both rulings demonstrate the lack of factual and legal basis for the allegations and emphasized that the company will continue to strengthen its compliance system and actively respond to unfounded lawsuits.

data.title: PBoC to Maintain Accommodative Monetary Policy, Says Governor Pan Gongsheng
data.description: The People's Bank of China (PBoC) will persist with an appropriately accommodative monetary policy in the upcoming phase, according to Governor Pan Gongsheng. According to NS3.AI, Pan emphasized the importance of enhancing both counter-cyclical and cross-cyclical adjustments. He also highlighted the need to establish a scientific and robust monetary policy framework to support economic stability.

data.title: Binance to Delist Multiple Tokens from Alpha Platform
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, several tokens will be removed from the Binance Alpha platform due to non-compliance with the platform's standards. The delisting is scheduled for 2026-03-12 at 12:00 (UTC). The affected tokens include MIRROR (Black Mirror Experience), SHARDS (WorldShards), FST (FreeStyle Classic), DGC (DecentralGPT), COA (Alliance Games), ULTI (Ultiverse), TGT (TOKYO GAMES TOKEN), AGON (AGON Agent), BNB Card (BNB Card), AFT (AIFlow), PFVS (Puffverse), SGC (SGC), RDO (Reddio), ELDE (Elderglade), MILK (MilkyWay), TAT (Tell A Tale), BOT (Hyperbot), SSS (Sparkle), SUBHUB (SubHub), PLANCK (Planck), and OOOO (oooo).

Despite the removal from the featuring list, Binance Alpha will still allow the selling of these tokens. Users can sell them by navigating to the [Market] tab in Binance Wallet or the [Asset] tab in Binance Alpha, selecting the token, and proceeding with the sale. Binance emphasizes the importance of user protection and transparency, urging users to exercise caution due to the potential high risk and price volatility associated with these tokens. Users are advised to conduct thorough research to understand the projects fully before engaging in any trading activities.

Binance remains committed to fostering innovation while ensuring user safety. The platform encourages users to stay informed by following Binance Wallet’s official X account for updates. The announcement also highlights Binance's regular review mechanism to maintain the quality of tokens listed on its platform and its efforts to enhance transparency and alert the community about fraudulent activities. Users are reminded to refer to the original English version of the announcement for the most accurate information.

data.title: EU countries will notify the International Energy Agency today of their intention to release oil reserves.
data.description: EU countries will notify the International Energy Agency (IEA) of their intention to release oil reserves by 16:00 GMT today (24:00 Beijing time). (Jinshi)

data.title: Analyst Predicts Continued Dollar Support Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Monex Europe analysts have indicated that the U.S. dollar is likely to remain supported in the short term unless there are credible signs of de-escalation in the Iran conflict. According to Jin10, the ongoing conflict is driving safe-haven flows towards the dollar. The war has led to rising oil prices, reinforcing market expectations that the Federal Reserve will maintain its tight monetary policy for a longer period than previously anticipated, thereby supporting the dollar. However, in the longer term, the market may be underestimating the potential extent of U.S. policy easing once concerns over energy prices begin to subside, which poses a downside risk to the dollar.

data.title: The compensation mechanism for the 61,000 Bitcoin money laundering case has been questioned, and Chinese victims have launched a legal challenge in the UK.
data.description: Victims of a Chinese investment fraud case are challenging a compensation arrangement involving approximately 61,000 bitcoins in a UK court. Representatives have filed an application with the High Court to reject a scheme using Chinese compensation mechanisms, arguing that it could allow British authorities to retain substantial gains from asset appreciation. The bitcoins, seized by British police in a money laundering investigation in London, are currently valued at approximately £3.2 billion (about $4.3 billion). The law firm Candey, representing about 5,700 victims, is reportedly objecting, arguing that the current compensation scheme does not guarantee fair compensation. The fraud, orchestrated by Qian Zhimin, involved over 128,000 Chinese investors, with proceeds converted into bitcoins and transferred overseas between 2014 and 2017. British prosecutors have stated that some claims could result in a minority of victims and those funding the litigation receiving compensation exceeding their actual losses. (FT)

data.title: Italy's Major Banks Aim to Revise Rescue Plan for Banca Progetto Amid Legal Concerns
data.description: Italy's largest banks are looking to amend a €750 million plan to rescue Oaktree Capital Management's Banca Progetto. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that recent developments in a criminal investigation have increased legal risks associated with the rescue effort. The lenders are reassessing their strategy in light of these new challenges, which could impact the financial stability and future operations of Banca Progetto. The situation underscores the complexities involved in navigating legal and financial landscapes in the banking sector.

data.title: Cosmos Health, a US-listed company, spent $600,000 to increase its Bitcoin holdings.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Cosmos Health announced it has invested $600,000 to increase its Bitcoin holdings, bringing its total digital asset portfolio to $3.1 million. In addition to Bitcoin, the company also holds Ethereum. (Globenewswire)

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum options with a notional value of $2.18 billion will expire and settle on March 13.
data.description: Bitcoin and Ethereum options with a notional value of $2.18 billion will expire and settle on March 13th. 26,000 BTC options will expire with a put-call ratio of 0.9, a maximum price threshold of $69,000, and a notional value of $1.8 billion; 182,000 ETH options will expire with a put-call ratio of 1.21, a maximum price threshold of $2,000, and a notional value of $380 million. Looking at options market data, the implied volatility (IV) for short-to-medium term options decreased this week, while the IV for medium-to-long term options increased, but by only a small margin. The IV for the main term of BTC is around 50%, and for ETH it is around 70%, with a more pronounced decrease in RV (Return on Investment).

data.title: Joel O’Leary Returns to Full-Time Work After Achieving Financial Independence
data.description: Joel O’Leary, who previously left his sales job at the age of 33 after achieving financial independence, has returned to full-time employment eight years later by pursuing an online writing career. According to NS3.AI, this transition has provided him with greater flexibility, reinstated employer health benefits, and alleviated the pressure to deplete his savings.
